title: Fermented Silky Raw-friendly Irish Whole Wheat Stir-fry
description: A one-pot dish that combines fermented vegetables with whole wheat and a tandoor-inspired flavor profile for a raw-friendly meal.
instructions: Soak wheat gluten in water for about 30 minutes.
Preheat your stove to medium-high heat. Season the wheat gluten with salt and pepper before stir-frying it until golden brown and crispy on a non-stick pan, then set aside.
Clean all vegetables and chop them finely: garlic, ginger, carrots, celery, green onions.
Add vegetable oil into a wok or large skillet, heat up to medium-high. Add fermented vegetables for about 2-3 minutes until fragrant.

Add wheat gluten, garlic, ginger, sliced green onions, chopped vegetables, and seasoning mix. Stir-fry everything together for another 5 minutes until all ingredients are heated through and fully combined.
